textPlain: Censorship in Germany is bigger than one would think. Many pages are blocked for different reasons. May it be copyright or political (like rt.com)

The workarounds on this page mostly suggest to use large public resolvers. Feature request (not sure if the author is on HN): it would be interesting to know which domains are blocked by 9.9.9.9, 1.1.1.1, and especially the new DNS4EU service.
More censorship is a good inventive to build really uncensorable protocols that ISPs can't mess with.

Ah telefonica… that’s Spanish, same company that every weekend blocks cloudfare in order to “avoid” football piracy. They don’t believe in laws, well they believe that laws are for the plebs not for them, the elites

I was quite surprised to hear the Germany had licensed "a private organization" to block websites with "No judges" and "no transparency", so I had to look that up.Obviously the truth is completely orthogonal to what's being presented in the article. The CUII is indeed a consortium of internet providers and copyright holders, and they do indeed coordinate the blocking of "websites that structurally infringe copyright", but it's not all executives in dark backrooms.As of July of this year (2025) all orders are ordered by a judge, and all final orders are published on the website[1]. Prior to this, they had an internal review subject to review by the "Federal Network Agency" (the FCC of Germany).So yeah, it's just regular old democratic Bureaucracy. Not a shady cabal of Authors? censoring your free speech. While the structure is there (a private organization with members being rights holders and network providers), the structure of the work is completely above board. It's an organization that exists essentially to implement law. That's not illegal or totalitarian, that's just society.[1]: https://cuii.info/en/recommendations/
It used to be armbands, now it's DNS.
That's 24 entries over the course of almost 5 years. Seems awfully little - especially if the OP apparently saw CUII blocks frequently enough to run a website to track them.
yeah exactly! gassing millions of human beings or forcing them to dig their own graves before shooting entire families is practically the same as a dns block list
